schemes.name: Mid-Day Meal Scheme
schemes.benefit: Free cooked meal on all school days
schemes.notes: Strongest pull factor for attendance. Missing MDM = child likely hungry.

schemes.name: Right to Education Act

conditions: out of school or irregular attendance

schemes.benefit: Free and compulsory education, free uniforms, free textbooks
schemes.notes: Mandates enrollment and attendance. Schools must track dropouts.

schemes.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
schemes.benefit: Rs 150-750/month depending on class and gender
schemes.notes: Delayed disbursement is common problem. Direct bank transfer to child/guardian account.

schemes.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for ST Students
schemes.benefit: Rs 150-750/month depending on class and gender
schemes.notes: Often requires tribal certificate which families may not have.

schemes.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Minority Students
schemes.notes: Requires minority community certificate. Community leaders can help with documentation.

schemes.name: Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya
schemes.benefit: Free residential schooling, meals, uniforms, books
schemes.notes: Most powerful intervention for girls at risk. Residential nature addresses multiple barriers. District has 3 KGBV centers with limited seats.

schemes.name: Free Cycle Distribution Scheme

conditions: enrolled in government upper primary school

schemes.notes: Addresses distance barrier for girls. Often delays of 6-12 months in distribution.

schemes.name: Child Labor Rehabilitation Program
schemes.benefit: Withdrawal from labor, special training, family compensation Rs 3000/month for 2 years
schemes.implementingAgency: District Child Labor Committee / Labor Department
schemes.notes: Requires complaint or identification by field worker. CWC must be involved. Employer can be prosecuted.

schemes.name: Child Welfare Committee Intervention

conditions: child in need of care and protection

schemes.benefit: Legal protection, shelter if needed, rehabilitation
schemes.notes: Powerful but requires documentation. Field worker can directly report to CWC. Response time 48-72 hours for urgent cases.

schemes.name: Scholarship for Disabled Students
schemes.benefit: Rs 200/month + priority access to special educators
schemes.notes: Requires disability certificate. Process can take 3-6 months. District hospital assessment needed.

schemes.name: Nutrition Rehabilitation Center
schemes.benefit: 30-day residential treatment, therapeutic food, health monitoring
schemes.notes: For severely malnourished children. ANM referral needed. Free treatment and food.

schemes.name: Anganwadi Supplementary Nutrition
schemes.notes: Immediate nutrition support. AWW (Anganwadi Worker) in every village. Can refer to NRC.

attendancePatterns.reason: Rabi and Kharif harvest seasons. Men migrate to Punjab/Haryana. Girls take household responsibilities.

successfulInterventions: Community volunteer monitors during migration season
Mahila samiti engagement to address household burden
pendingActions: ASHA worker coordination for health issues

attendancePatterns.reason: Brick kiln season. Entire families including children work at kilns. High domestic violence rate.

successfulInterventions: Brick kiln owner meetings and awareness
Seasonal bridge school at kiln site
pendingActions: Domestic violence shelter tie-up with SNHU
communityPatterns.alerts: HIGH RISK - 3 active CWC cases. Domestic violence hotspot. Coordinate with police.

attendancePatterns.reason: Marriage season. Social pressure for early marriage peaks in winter. Also some post-harvest migration.

pendingActions: 2 girls at high marriage risk identified this month

attendancePatterns.reason: Monsoon makes road to school difficult. Also illness due to waterborne diseases.

successfulInterventions: Cycle distribution reduced dropout for girls by 15%
KGBV enrollment for 4 girls last year

attendancePatterns.reason: Caste-based discrimination causes irregular attendance. Child labor in domestic settings.

successfulInterventions: Anti-child labor awareness with women's groups

attendancePatterns.reason: Forest collection season. Tribal families collect forest produce. Children help.
Two major harvest seasons. Agricultural community.

successfulInterventions: Farmer community meetings about education value
pendingActions: Board exam attendance tracking for class 8 students

attendancePatterns.reason: Several orphan children with elderly guardians. Attendance disrupted when guardian is ill.

successfulInterventions: KGBV enrollment considered for orphan girls
Community foster arrangement in one case
pendingActions: Identify residential options for orphan children with unstable guardians

attendancePatterns.reason: Children with disabilities face access and inclusion barriers.
Relatively stable community with engaged parents.

successfulInterventions: Peer mentorship program with top students

immediate: Document evidence of child labor (photographs if safe, written notes)
File complaint with District Child Labor Committee (DCLC)
Report to Child Welfare Committee (CWC) for protection
Inform school headmaster to maintain enrollment
Engage with employer to stop child labor - warn of legal consequences
mediumTerm: Support family with Child Labor Rehabilitation compensation (Rs 3000/month)
Enroll child in bridge course or special training if learning gaps exist
Connect family with alternative livelihood support
Regular follow-up visits every 2 weeks
escalation: If employer continues: file FIR under Child Labor Act
If family uncooperative: CWC can take child into care
Coordinate with local police child protection unit

immediate: Engage with family - emphasize legal consequences (PCMA - child marriage is criminal offense)
Connect girl with KGBV residential school if eligible
Mobilize Mahila Samiti and women's groups for community pressure
Alert Block Education Officer if marriage is imminent
mediumTerm: Enroll girl in KGBV or residential facility
Engage adolescent girls group for peer support
Support family economically through schemes to reduce 'burden' perception
Sensitization sessions with extended family
escalation: Report to District Child Protection Officer if marriage date is set
Police complaint if marriage ceremony is being organized

immediate: Ensure immediate safety of child - assess severity
Connect mother with One Stop Center (OSC) for support
Report to CWC if child is in danger
Document injuries and incidents
Inform DCPO (District Child Protection Officer)
mediumTerm: Legal aid for mother through District Legal Services Authority
Psychological counseling for child through ICPS support
Consider KGBV or other residential option for child's safety
Engage with father through counseling if safe to do so
escalation: File NCR or FIR if violence is severe
Shelter placement through OSC or short-stay home
Protection order under PWDVA (Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act)

immediate: Register family with migration monitoring system
Ensure enrollment certificate is portable (Migration Certificate)
Connect with field worker at destination village if possible
mediumTerm: Seasonal bridge school at migration destination
Community volunteer to monitor children during parent migration
Engage with self-help groups to organize alternative childcare
escalation: Escalate if children are taken along with migrating parents and drop out completely

immediate: Refer to ANM (Auxiliary Nurse Midwife) for immediate health assessment
Ensure child is receiving MDM (Mid-Day Meal) regularly
Connect with Anganwadi Worker for supplementary nutrition
Check if SAM (Severe Acute Malnutrition) - refer to NRC if yes
mediumTerm: Monthly weight monitoring
Iron-Folic Acid supplements through ICDS
Family nutrition counseling
Connect with POSHAN Abhiyan local committee
escalation: Failure to thrive despite intervention: report to CMHO

immediate: Verify scholarship amount and disbursement status from department records
Check if child has own bank account or Aadhaar-linked account
Document the diversion with specific amounts and dates
mediumTerm: Open/link child's own bank account for direct transfer
Report to Social Welfare Officer / District Welfare Officer
Involve women's SHG to support family against moneylender pressure
escalation: Complaint to DM office if government officials complicit
Report moneylender for illegal interest to District administration
Legal aid for family if debt is bonded labor
